macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stock Indexes Mixed on Monday Amid Oil Price Surge and Iran Conflict Uncertainty
items.summary: The S&P 500 fell 0.4% to 6,343.72, Nasdaq dropped 0.7% to 20,794.64, while Dow edged up 0.1% to 45,216.14; markets cautious as oil tops $100/barrel due to war with Iran, deepening S&P's yearly loss to 7.3%.[1][2][3]

items.title: S&P 500 Enters Oversold Territory Below Key Moving Averages
items.summary: S&P 500 closed below declining trend channel and all major daily moving averages, with RSI under 30 signaling potential selling exhaustion amid parabolic decline linked to Iran conflict uncertainty.[2]

items.title: Russell 2000 Small-Cap Index Leads Decliners with 1.5% Drop
items.summary: Russell 2000 fell 1.5% to 2,414.01, reflecting broader market de-risking; year-to-date down 2.7% as caution prevails in equities.[1]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Ethereum Price Surges to $2,070.90 with $70 Daily Gain
items.summary: As of 8:45 a.m. ET on March 30, 2026, Ethereum reached $2,070.90, up $70.70 from the previous day and $264 from a year ago, amid volatile market swings. Bitcoin traded at $67,822.72, while XRP stood at $1.35.

items.title: Bitcoin Everlight Advances Phase 3 Presale Amid US-Iran Tensions
items.summary: Bitcoin Everlight, a transaction routing network parallel to Bitcoin, reports ongoing Phase 3 presale at $0.0012 per BTCL token, having raised over $2 million total. The project offers fixed rewards transitioning to performance-based BTC distribution at mainnet launch.

items.title: March 2026 Marks Crypto's Major Regulatory Shift
items.summary: March 2026 brought the biggest crypto regulatory changes since the Bitcoin ETF approval, featuring clearer rules, better agency alignment, and boosted institutional adoption pathways.

items.title: Bitcoin Hits Critical $66,200 Support Level
items.summary: Bitcoin approaches a pivotal $66,200 support amid key price scenarios and liquidation pools, as analyzed in the March 31 morning summary.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Scientists Develop Ultra-Robust Machine-Learning Models for Stable Molecular Simulations
items.summary: Researchers have created the first AI-powered model using Gaussian process regression that maintains stable molecular simulations under extreme conditions, preventing molecules from breaking apart. This breakthrough enables reliable long-term studies for drug development and materials science without supercomputers.

items.title: Meta Tests Avocado 9B and Avocado Mango Agent Models Amid Delays
items.summary: Meta is experimenting with Avocado 9B and other variants, which solve complex math problems but lag behind competitors, pushing the full Avocado release to at least May. The company is reportedly licensing Google's Gemini technology for some Meta AI requests.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Major Indie Game Releases This Week Including March 31
items.summary: A mega week of indie game releases is underway, with numerous titles launching on March 31, 2026, such as Sprint City, Subliminal, Super Meat Boy 3D, Tombwater, Truck Mechanic: Dangerous Paths, Voodoo Fishin', Wasteland Bites, Xenonauts 2, and Zero Stress King: Idle Defense. Expectations for April Fool's surprises from developers like Landfall.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Senator Wiener Announces Landmark Legislation To Crack Down on Big Tech's Anticompetitive Behavior
items.summary: Senator Wiener introduced significant legislation targeting anticompetitive practices by major technology companies, marking a major regulatory push against Big Tech.

items.title: US Regulator Bans Imports of New Foreign-Made Routers Over Security Concerns
items.summary: U.S. regulators have implemented a ban on imports of new foreign-manufactured routers citing national security risks.

items.title: Supreme Court Sides With Internet Provider in Copyright Fight Over Pirated Music
items.summary: The Supreme Court ruled in favor of an internet provider in a copyright dispute involving pirated music distribution.

items.title: Semiconductor Sector Extends Losing Streak Amid Tech Selloff
items.summary: The semiconductor industry continues to decline, with Micron Technology down more than 30% since March 18th as part of a broader tech sector selloff.

items.title: Infinite Campus Warns of Data Breach After ShinyHunters Claims Data Theft
items.summary: Education software provider Infinite Campus disclosed a security breach following claims by the ShinyHunters group of data theft.

items.title: Cyberattack on Car Breathalyzer Firm Leaves Drivers Stuck
items.summary: A cyberattack targeting a car breathalyzer company has disrupted service, leaving drivers unable to use the affected systems.
